No Poverty.reason: The course explicitly discusses poverty eradication through the reading "How China Escaped the Poverty Trap" (中國如何跳出貧困陷阱), linking to Target 1.b.
evidence: 中國如何跳出貧困陷阱

Quality Education.reason: The course discusses the "cultivation of officials" (官員的養成) and political schools, which relates to specialized training, but not broadly to quality education for all (Target 4.4).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers "Reform and opening up" (改革開放) and "industrial and commercial taxation" (工商稅收), directly addressing policies for economic growth (Target 8.1).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course touches upon "base area construction" (根據地建設) and "building the nation" (建國), which inferentially involves infrastructure and industrialization (Targets 9.1, 9.2).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course analyzes political ideologies and "conflict" (衝突), which are fundamentally about social, economic, and political inclusion/exclusion, linking to Targets 10.2 and 10.3.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Discusses governance in cities like "Tianjin and Shanghai" (天津和上海), which tangentially relates to human settlement management (Target 11.3), but lacks a sustainability focus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is a core study of "political forces," "conflict," "civil war," and "governance," directly analyzing the formation of institutions, linking to Targets 16.1, 16.3, and 16.6.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course adopts an "international perspective" (國際觀視野) to understand political forces affecting "global destiny" (全球命運), promoting knowledge sharing and policy coherence (Targets 17.16, 17.14).
evidence: 解讀國內外重要學術著作
